exemplar.title: This investigation seeks to model the volume of a bottle of 1.54 litre âSimply Orangeâ orange juice by using the method of solids of revolution, and thereby comparing the volume determined through methods of calculus and the actual volume.

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Title clearly identifies the subject and level, orienting the reader immediately
Complete table of contents with numbered headings aids navigation
Well-organized sections (Introduction, Method, Results, Evaluation, Conclusion)
weaknesses: Long digressions on basic calculus theory interrupt the logical flow
Bibliography is unnumbered and some headings duplicate information
Repetition and lengthy quotations reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall the structure is clear and the reader can follow the argument, but unnecessary material and occasional digressions obscure the logical thread. This places the work solidly in the âsome coherenceâ band rather than a higher band of presentation.

strengths: Derivations are shown in a readable sequence with explanatory sentences
Figures (e.g., Figure 3) and tables are well integrated and support the text
Integration rules and step-by-step accumulation of volumes are clearly communicated
weaknesses: Occasional notation slips (cmÂ² vs cm2, omitted differentials) reduce precision
Some figures lack scales on axes, hindering interpretation
Jumps in reasoning (e.g., generic washer formula to piece-wise integrals) limit absolute clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematical argument is generally well structured and uses appropriate notation and multiple representations, placing it in the âgood logical structureâ band. Minor inconsistencies and clarity issues prevent a top mark.

strengths: Clear personal anecdote motivating the research question reflects genuine interest
Studentâs choice of a familiar bottle and custom piece-wise model shows independent thinking
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques remain standard and rely on Desmos outputs
Creativity is present but confined to routine quadratic fits and manual measurements
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates significant evidence of independent thinking and a clear personal approach, but it lacks the depth of innovation or sophisticated methods required for the highest band.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on choice of solids of revolution and measurement challenges
Identification of sources of error (rounding, ruler precision) with qualitative discussion
weaknesses: Reflection remains descriptive rather than deeply quantitative
Limited discussion of future improvements or rigorous validation methods
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is meaningful and the student evaluates outcomes clearly, but the analysis does not reach the substantial critical depth or propose detailed future implications needed for the top band.

strengths: All mathematics used (definite integrals, scaling factors) is directly relevant
Step-by-step accumulation of volumes is methodical and accurate
Cross-sectional area scaling explanation shows clear understanding of unit conversion
weaknesses: Minor algebraic slips and inconsistent units cause small discrepancies
Typographical errors (e.g., incorrect square factor) need correction
No use of higher-level or more sophisticated HL mathematical tools
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is appropriate to SL course level and demonstrates good understanding with only minor errors. It does not reach the comprehensive or expert level required for a higher mark, placing it firmly at the middle band.

strandSelections.reasoning: Steps within derivations are shown in a readable order and linked with explanatory sentences, so the mathematical argument can be followed without excessive effort.  A few jumpsâsuch as moving from the generic washer formula to piece-wise integrals without explicit justificationâlimit absolute clarity, but overall logical structure is good.

Step-by-step antiderivatives are mostly correct and numerical results are plausible.  Some algebraic slips (e.g., mis-copying coefficients, inconsistent units) lead to slight discrepancies between tables and text, indicating good but not thorough understanding.  Hence the âgood understanding with minor errorsâ descriptor is appropriate.

The student provides graphs with superimposed images, colour-coded pieces, tables of numerical results and a photograph validating the measurement.  These multiple representations are integrated into the text and clearly referenced, supporting the mathematics effectively, though some figures are low resolution and axes lack scales, preventing a higher band.

The exploration follows the sequence IntroductionâMethodâResultsâEvaluation, and ideas are generally linked in a way that lets the reader follow the argument.  Nevertheless, long digressions on basic calculus theory break the flow and occasionally obscure the main purpose, so the logical thread is only partially maintained.  This places the work solidly in the âsome coherenceâ descriptor rather than the consistently coherent band.

Breaking the complex shape into multiple functions, devising a scaling procedure and extending the study to find the half-volume height show a reasonable level of initiative.  Creativity is present but limited by routine use of quadratic fits and manual ruler measurements rather than more sophisticated fitting or digital tools.

Sources of error (rounding, ruler precision, omitted indentations) are identified and their potential impacts qualitatively discussed, showing thoughtful analysis.  The analysis does not quantify error margins or propose rigorous validation, so it is thoughtful but not âdeep and insightfulâ.

The student compares calculated and measured volumes and states that the difference is acceptable, commenting on practical implications for splitting the juice.  This constitutes a clear evaluation, yet future implications are only briefly touched upon, preventing a move to the highest band.

The student reflects on why solids of revolution were chosen and comments on measurement challenges, demonstrating meaningful consideration.  Reflection occurs at several points (Evaluation, Comparison) but is descriptive rather than deeply critical, so it fits the âmeaningfulâ band.

Mathematical symbols, function notation, integral bounds and units are generally correct and used throughout.  There are occasional slipsâe.g., switching between cmÂ² and cm2 or omitting differentials in some displayed integralsâbut the language remains mostly consistent and suitable for HL level.

The narrative of family disputes over sharing juice provides a clear personal context, and the student tailors the investigation (e.g., deciding which bottle features to ignore) to that context, demonstrating a personal approach.  The approach is evident but not highly innovative, keeping it in band 2.

Most sections relate directly to determining the volume, yet sizeable portions (e.g., a full page on basic limit notation and the cylinder-slicing derivation) add little to answering the research question.  Repetition of scaling explanations and lengthy quotations also reduce conciseness.  Thus the content is mostly relevant but includes noticeable unnecessary material.

All mathematics usedâarea under curves, definite integrals, scaling factorsâis directly relevant to modelling the bottle.  No superfluous techniques are included, so relevance is clear.  The work, however, does not advance beyond standard calculus, so it is appropriately relevant rather than comprehensive or expert.

Selecting a personally relevant bottle and devising a custom piece-wise model shows independent decisions beyond classroom exemplars.  However, the mathematical techniques themselves remain standard (basic integration and scaling) and rely heavily on Desmos outputs, so the evidence is significant but not outstanding.

A complete table of contents, numbered headings, and clear separation of neck/body modelling show that the student has planned a recognisable structure.  Introduction, evaluation and conclusion are present and placed logically.  Minor issuesâsuch as the bibliography appearing unnumbered and some headings duplicating informationâprevent the structure from being exemplary, but it is definitely clear and well-organised.

Solids of revolution and definite integration are core HL/SL content and therefore commensurate with course level.  The student handles piece-wise functions and scaling, showing appropriate application, but there is no use of higher-level HL topics such as parametric surfaces or numerical methods, so the work remains in the middle band.

annotations.comment: Axis labels and units are missing in this description; explicitly state which axis corresponds to real measurements to improve clarity.
The description of superimposing and rotating the image could be more concise; consider summarizing key steps and moving details into a methodological appendix.
Figure 3 is well integrated with the text, showing the Desmos modeling clearly and supporting the mathematical process.
Explain the rationale for choosing four separate functions to model the neck; justify segment boundaries to strengthen the mathematical approach.
The table lists a coefficient of -0.3846 for the green function, but later text uses -0.4846; ensure consistency in numerical data.
The explanation jumps from generic washer formula to piecewise integrals without explicit transition; add justification for splitting and new bounds.
Axes in Figure 5 lack scales or tick marks, making it difficult to interpret segment heights directly from the diagram.
Clarify the transition to the trapezoidal-style approximation in $$V=\pi\bigl(\frac{f(x_c)+f(x_{c+1})}{2}\bigr)^2h$$ and relate it explicitly to the limiting integral.
The factor \(h\) should be taken outside the integral \(\int_a^b\pi f(x)^2dx\) rather than inside, to reflect constant section thickness.
The integration rules section concisely outlines the power rule, effectively supporting subsequent calculations.
The function notation used here (green line) does not match earlier values; revise to ensure all symbolic definitions align.
Limits of integration are set to 0â0.6 here, but earlier you indicated 0â0.744 for the first segment; correct the bounds.
The title clearly identifies the subject and level, immediately orienting the reader to the exploration.
Number the bibliography entry in the table of contents and verify page numbering consistency to enhance navigability.
The personal anecdote engagingly motivates the research question and reflects genuine interest, showing clear personal engagement.
The figure is properly sourced and integrates visual support effectively, aiding communication of the real object.
The aim is concisely stated, linking the method to the research question and clarifying the investigationâs purpose.
This section provides extensive basic calculus theory that is not directly needed; condense or relocate to an appendix for conciseness.
Summation of quarterâbase areas in Table 5 is clearly executed, demonstrating consistent application of integration to the body.
The computed halfâheight of 9.45 cm slightly differs from the measured 9.6 cm; discuss potential sources (e.g., rounding, neglected indentations).
When comparing volumes, specify that 1565 mL equals 1565 cmÂ³ to avoid potential unit confusion.
There is a typographical error â24Â²â instead of â1.24Â²â when squaring the scale factor; correct to avoid confusion.
Add superscripts to cm in the table header (e.g., cmÂ²) for clarity and consistency in units.
The stepâbyâstep accumulation of volumes in Table 3 is methodical and clearly communicates the segmental approach.
The final assembly of neck volume is succinctly presented, reinforcing the accuracy of the segmental integration process.
The evaluation identifies key measurement and modeling limitations but would benefit from proposing specific quantitative errorâreduction strategies.
The crossâsectional area scaling explanation is well reasoned, showing clear understanding of unit conversion.
